title: Adobe Acrobat - Getting Started (UW-Madison)
body: Eligibility, Install, and Sign In Faculty and staff can download Acrobat by following the directions in Adobe Creative Cloud - Logging in with Your UW-Madison Account . Other paid UW positions are also eligible. See the full eligibility list here - UW System Eligibility for Adobe Creative Cloud . Students do NOT automatically get access to Acrobat. If you need access to Acrobat, please check our student program page - Adobe Creative Cloud - Student Subscription FAQ Acrobat is part of Creative Cloud and we strongly recommend installing and launching it through the Creative Cloud app. You can check the download instructions on our login and download page - Adobe Creative Cloud - Logging in with Your UW-Madison Account Adobe Acrobat has been licensed by the UW-System for use by faculty and staff on both UW- and personally-owned devices, as well as those with UW-owned machines provisioned with Shared Device Licensing. Please check with your local IT staff to see if they can install Adobe Acrobat for you or if there are departmental policies on use and support of Adobe products. You are licensed to have Creative Cloud activated on up to 2 computers. Tutorials Adobe Education Exchange Acrobat materials Adobe Acrobat video tutorials Adobe Acrobat Learn and Support Lynda.com and LinkedIn Learning have excellent tutorials on Adobe Acrobat. For a broad overview see Up and Running with Acrobat XI . Note that while you can download and install Acrobat XI at the time this kb document was written, you can also download and install Acrobat DC which looks somewhat different than Acrobat XI but includes the same functionality. You can also find many YouTube tutorials on Adobe Acrobat . Documentation and Resources Adobe Acrobat help documents Adobe Acrobat manual / reference Adobe Acrobat community UW Madison Resources If you are a UW-Madison student and would like assistance with using Adobe Acrobat please contact DoIT STS through their "Ask a Trainer" service . Someone will contact you within two business days to schedule a consultation. Instructors, staff, and students can use the Adobe software suite at most UW-Madison computer labs . If you are a student and need access, please check our student program page - Adobe Creative Cloud - Student Subscription FAQ Note: To get started with Acrobat, you will need to log in with the campus Adobe license: Adobe Creative Cloud - Logging in with Your UW-Madison Account . See Also Adobe Creative Cloud - Student Subscription FAQ Eligibility for Adobe Creative Cloud Licensed By University of Wisconsin System's ETLA Adobe Creative Cloud - Logging in with Your UW-Madison Account
